codeReview.summary: The feature adds substantial code-execution scaffolding and tests, but two scoped requirements are not fully met: five listed existing expert problems were duplicated instead of converted in place, and one problem's test cases do not provide boundary/edge coverage.

issues.description: The 5 listed existing expert problems (topological_sort, constrained_factorial, code_with_comments, library_schema, adversarial_transform) were added as new cloned entries in _CROSS_CATEGORY_EXPERT_CODING instead of converting their existing definitions. The original problems in reasoning_problems.py:441 and instruction_problems.py:228/245/260/277 still have no function_signature/test_cases, so expert/all benchmark paths continue to evaluate those originals via pattern checks rather than code execution.

_LIBRARY_SCHEMA_TEST_CASES repeats the same empty input and identical expected_output five times (lines 1314, 1348, 1382, 1416, 1450), which does not satisfy the requirement to include boundary/edge cases for each problem.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared guidance does not define how to convert cross-category coding-style problems while preserving backward-compatible tier lists, which led to an in-flight strategy change and the duplicate-list workaround.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows: initial integration caused backward-compat test failures (duplicate expert entries), then worker refactored to keep originals and add _CROSS_CATEGORY_EXPERT_CODING only for CODING_PROBLEMS.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Architecture shared-state docs are out of sync with the actual code layout and interfaces.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md lines 25-26 and 60 describe a problems/ package and CodeExecutionProblem type, but mtb/quality_benchmarks/ currently uses flat modules and no CodeExecutionProblem class.

summary: Review failed with 2 blocking findings: (1) five named existing expert problems were not converted in place for runtime code-execution paths, and (2) library_schema test cases lack boundary/edge variation. Report written for scrutiny triage.
